title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Education

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Skills & Coursework
Technical Skills: R; STATA; Python; Julia; LaTeX; Salesforce; Microsoft Office; Optical Character Recognition; Census Matching; Panel and Cross-sectional Econometrics; DSGE Modeling; Randomized Control Trials
Languages: English (Native); French (B1); Khmer/Cambodian (Basic Work Proficiency)
Graduate Coursework: Advanced Macroeconomics I, II, III; Advanced Microeconomics I, II; Advanced Econometrics I, II, III; Development Economics; Public Economics; Economic History; Computational Economics; Political Economy; International Economics

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Professional Experience
Penn Wharton Budget Model · Philadelphia, United States Analyst (Full-Time) · June 2026 – Present
Department of Economics, Trinity College Dublin · Dublin, Ireland Predoctoral Research Assistant (Full-Time) · September 2025 – June 2026

Consulted on data processing and econometric methodology for research papers
Developed OCR (Python), census matching and cleaning (STATA) models to generate a large database of Russian Empire administrative and business surveys
Built webscraping models (Python) to construct databases of 100,000 foreigners in pre-revolution Russia and to extract tables and graphs from House Commons Papers
Developed VLM- and LLM-based OCR pipelines to convert historical PDFs into usable data
Tested and improved OCR models on the 1845–1914 Thom’s Street Directory reports from an HPC

The Economic Journal, Royal Economic Society · Paris, France Replicator (Part-Time) · August 2024 – June 2025

Replicated journal paper submissions under the supervision of Dr. Florian Oswald
Identified errors in submission code and provided recommendations

Kiel Institute for the World Economy · Remote Research Intern (Full-Time) · July 2024 – September 2024

Supported research by Dr. Christoph Trebesch and Dr. Johannes Marzian on militarization, public finance, and geopolitics; contributed to a working paper
Collected, cleaned, and analyzed historical public finance data for 20+ advanced economies (1870–2020) in R and STATA
Produced 200+ visualizations for exploratory analysis

Ceres, Inc. · Remote Financial Regulation Intern (Full-Time) · June 2023 – August 2023

Produced reports on international, federal, and state financial regulation policies
Wrote an 11-page internal report on improving the Federal Reserve’s climate-risk scenario analysis

Bard College Department of Economics · Annandale, United States Economics Tutor (Part-Time) · March 2022 – May 2023
Mekong Strategic Group · Phnom Penh, Cambodia Analytics Intern (Full-Time) · June 2022 – August 2022
OSUN – Economic Democracy Initiative · Annandale, United States Student Fellow (Full-Time) · June 2021 – August 2021
Office of Illinois State Senator Heather Steans · Chicago, United States Summer Intern (Full-Time) · June 2020 – August 2020

title: Research
text: I investigate whether renewable energy dependence can shield counties from higher energy prices stemming from exogenous oil price shocks.

title: Research
text: I investigate whether renewable energy dependence can shield counties from higher energy prices stemming from exogenous oil price shocks.

title: Eli Cheam Shapiro
text:
Eli Cheam Shapiro
    Incoming Analyst, Penn Wharton Budget Model
    MRes in Economics · Sciences Po Paris

    
      In June, I will be joining the
      Penn Wharton Budget Model
      as an analyst. My research interests are fiscal policy, public investment, and inequality.
    

    
      Previously, I was a Predoctoral Research Assistant at Trinity College Dublin.
      I hold a Master of Research in Economics from Sciences Po Paris
      and a BA in Economics from Bard College.
    

    
      I am proficient in R, Python, STATA, and Julia.
